content:
# Revelation Chapter 19 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, pages 291-292 | **Source Image:** REV_019_page_001.jpg, REV_019_page_002.jpg

**1.** After this I heard what sounded like the roar of a vast crowd in heaven, shouting: "Hallelujah! Salvation, glory, and power belong to our God,

**2.** because his judgments are true and just. He has condemned the great prostitute who was poisoning the earth with her sexual immorality. He has avenged the blood of his servants that was on her hands."

**3.** And a second time they shouted: "Hallelujah! The smoke from her burning rises forever and ever!"

**4.** Then the twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshiped God, who sits on the throne, crying out: "Amen! Hallelujah!"

**5.** And a voice came from the throne, saying: "Praise our God, all you who serve him -- all who stand in awe of him, from the least to the greatest!"

**6.** Then I heard what sounded like the roar of an enormous crowd, like the thunder of mighty waters, like the crash of powerful thunder, shouting: "Hallelujah! For the Lord our God, the Almighty, has begun his reign!

**7.** Let us rejoice and be overjoyed and give him glory! For the wedding celebration of the Lamb has arrived, and his bride has made herself ready.

**8.** She has been given brilliant, clean linen to wear" -- for the fine linen represents the righteous deeds of God's holy people.

**9.** Then the angel said to me, "Write this down: Blessed are those who are invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb." And he added, "These are the true words of God."

**10.** I fell at his feet to worship him, but he said to me, "Do not do that! I am a fellow servant with you and with your brothers and sisters who hold to the testimony of Jesus. Worship God!" For the testimony of Jesus is the spirit that animates all prophecy.

**11.** Then I saw heaven standing open, and there before me was a white horse. Its rider is called Faithful and True, and he judges and wages war with perfect justice.

**12.** His eyes blaze like fire, and on his head are many royal crowns. He has a name written on him that no one knows except himself.

**13.** He is dressed in a robe dipped in blood, and his name is the Word of God.

**14.** The armies of heaven followed him, riding white horses and dressed in fine linen, white and clean.

**15.** From his mouth extends a sharp sword with which to strike down the nations. He will rule them with an iron scepter. He treads the winepress of the furious wrath of God the Almighty.

**16.** On his robe and on his thigh he has this name written: KING OF KINGS AND LORD OF LORDS.

**17.** Then I saw an angel standing in the sun, and he cried out in a loud voice to all the birds flying high overhead: "Come! Gather for God's great feast!

**18.** Come and devour the flesh of kings, the flesh of generals, the flesh of the powerful, the flesh of horses and their riders, and the flesh of all people -- free and slave, small and great alike."

**19.** Then I saw the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies, assembled to wage war against the rider on the horse and his army.

**20.** But the beast was captured, and with it the false prophet who had performed signs on its behalf -- the signs by which he had deceived those who received the mark of the beast and worshiped its image. The two of them were thrown alive into the lake of fire that burns with sulfur.

**21.** The rest were killed by the sword that extended from the mouth of the rider on the horse, and all the birds gorged themselves on their flesh.

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

This chapter moves from cosmic liturgy to cosmic warfare. My translation aims to preserve both the hymnic beauty of the opening and the terrifying violence of the conclusion:

- **"Roar of a vast crowd" (v. 1):** The Greek *phonon megalon ochlou pollou* conveys not just volume but massiveness -- a sound that fills all of heaven. "Roar" captures the visceral, overwhelming quality.

- **"Poisoning the earth" (v. 2):** The Greek *ephtheiren ten gen* means "was corrupting/destroying the earth." I chose "poisoning" because it captures the insidious, pervasive nature of the corruption -- Babylon's influence seeped into everything it touched.

- **"The roar... the thunder... the crash" (v. 6):** The triple simile (*hos phonon ochlou pollou, hos phonon hydaton pollon, hos phonon bronton ischuron*) builds from crowd noise to water to thunder. I vary the introductory words ("roar," "thunder," "crash") to prevent monotony while preserving the escalating intensity.

- **"Has begun his reign" (v. 6):** The aorist *ebasileusen* is an ingressive aorist -- it marks the commencement of God's sovereign rule, not merely a statement that God reigns. "Has begun his reign" captures this pivotal moment.

- **"The spirit that animates all prophecy" (v. 10):** The Greek *to pneuma tes propheteias* ("the spirit of prophecy") is clarified here -- the testimony about Jesus is the animating force, the very breath, behind all true prophetic speech.

- **"Dipped in blood" (v. 13):** The Sinaiticus reads *bebammenon haimati* ("dipped/dyed in blood"). This is blood present before the battle begins, likely alluding to the rider's own sacrificial blood (echoing Isaiah 63), not the blood of enemies. The ambiguity is preserved by the neutral "dipped."

- **"God's great feast" (v. 17):** The Greek *to deipnon to mega tou theou* ("the great supper of God") deliberately mirrors and inverts the "wedding feast of the Lamb" from verse 9. One feast celebrates union; the other celebrates destruction. I use "feast" for both to maintain the grim parallel.

- **"Gorged themselves" (v. 21):** The Greek *echortasthesan* means "were filled to satisfaction." "Gorged" conveys the grotesque excess of this anti-feast, completing the dark inversion of the marriage supper.

-- PNEUMA
